rationale: The only substantive ground-truth concern (yJader's inline comment explaining that submit_initial and submit_update both reject list prompts, so gating the unwrap on already_submitted was wrong) is reconstructed almost verbatim by Y, citing the exact ValueError raises in stage_pool.py for both submit paths. X only gestures at this ('fixing the regression... on the non-already_submitted path') without articulating the parity reasoning. X also undermines its own precision: it explicitly validates that already_submitted 'remains used in other conditions... not dead' in its sweep notes, then contradicts itself by posting a comment asking to check if the variable is dead code, and it asserts unverified DiffusionOutput fields (.finished, .request_id) not evidenced anywhere in its own cited code. Y's comments are more consistent and tightly grounded in the diff and surrounding code it read. Both add reasonable but non-critical test-coverage suggestions; neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
